name: BOOK_CONSOLIDATION
description: Swarm manuscript consolidation workflow. Forked from RDC_WORKFLOW. Transforms a chaotic folder of documents — fragments, drafts, notes, partial chapters of varying quality and epoch — into a structured manuscript with chapter organization and section hierarchy. Sequential temporal-upsert of N source documents into a running MASTER, with COURT mechanism for conflict resolution, then COMPOSITOR carves MASTER into ordered chapter files. Output feeds BOOK_STORYBOARD. Triggered by the phrase 'BOOK_CONSOLIDATION'. Owner: Michael. Orchestrator: QUEEN (Claude in main conversation, not a spawned agent).

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN reads BOOK_MANIFEST.json from project root
QUEEN reads chapter_subset parameter (from invocation or from BOOK_MANIFEST.json workflow_settings; invocation takes precedence; if absent or null = full-manuscript pass)
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_CONSOLIDATION mode engaged. Ready. Point me to the source directory.'
QUEEN waits for explicit human direction specifying the source directory path
trigger.source_directory_requires: explicit human direction specifying the directory containing source documents

chapter_subset_parameter.format: null | [array of chapter slugs] | {from: slug, to: slug}
chapter_subset_parameter.source_precedence: invocation parameter overrides BOOK_MANIFEST.json workflow_settings; absent/null = full-manuscript pass
chapter_subset_parameter.behavior_when_present: SCRIBE_LOOP processes only source files mapped to the subset chapters (via triage.per_chapter_state[i].source_files); COMPOSITOR carves only those chapters from MASTER; QA_COMPLETENESS and QA_COHERENCE scope to the subset chapters and their source files
chapter_subset_parameter.validation: QUEEN verifies each slug in subset exists in chapters/ (if pre-existing) or in scope.intended_chapters; invalid slugs block workflow
chapter_subset_parameter.unselected_chapters_preserved: STRUCTURE.md entries and chapter files for non-subset chapters are not modified if they exist from a prior run

relationship_to_book_family.contract: BOOK_CONSOLIDATION produces structured chapter files and STRUCTURE.md. BOOK_STORYBOARD operates on these to produce the logical skeleton. Manual handoff — human reviews output before triggering BOOK_STORYBOARD.

deltas: COMPOSITOR replaces TAXONOMIST — output is chapter files + STRUCTURE.md instead of PROJECT/ARCH/TODO
QA_COHERENCE adapted for manuscript structure instead of engineering structure
Output files are chapters/ directory + STRUCTURE.md
BOOK_MANIFEST.json read at engagement for genre/structure context
All other mechanics (SCRIBE, COURT, ADVOCATE, PEDAGOGY, EVALUATIONS, INVENTORY, upsert rules) ported directly from RDC

document_conventions.source_docs: *.md files (and other text formats) in the designated source directory
document_conventions.master_description: Running consolidated document built by sequential SCRIBE passes. Represents the current best-known state of all content from source documents. Grows and mutates with each SCRIBE pass. Intermediate representation from which chapter files are carved. Preserved at project root after GREEN_LIGHT for reference.
document_conventions.master_update_detail: SCRIBE reads current MASTER + next source doc. Concepts already in MASTER are updated in place if the source doc contains newer/revised information. New concepts are inserted in the most structurally appropriate location. No concept is deleted — only overwritten with evolved versions. Concepts that were court-resolved carry a back-reference to the INPROGRESS.md court entry.
document_conventions.pedagogy_description: Archaeological record of concept evolution. Every time SCRIBE overwrites or significantly revises a concept in MASTER, the prior value, new value, source doc, and reasoning are logged here. Append-only.

pedagogy_entry_format.timestamp: SCRIBE pass number + source doc filename
pedagogy_entry_format.concept: name/path of the concept that changed
pedagogy_entry_format.prior_value: what MASTER said before this pass
pedagogy_entry_format.new_value: what MASTER says after this pass
pedagogy_entry_format.reason: why the change was made (derived from source doc context)
pedagogy_entry_format.court_reference: optional — INPROGRESS.md anchor if this change was court-ruled

document_conventions.inventory_description: Manifest of all source documents with temporal ordering, produced during INVENTORY phase.
document_conventions.evaluation_description: Per-document evaluation produced by SCRIBE during each pass. Append-only.
document_conventions.structure_description: Manuscript skeleton produced by COMPOSITOR. Contains: table of contents, chapter summaries, section listings per chapter, inter-chapter dependency map. The structural reference document for all downstream workflows.
document_conventions.chapter_output_description: One file per discovered chapter. Numbered for ordering. Contains section hierarchy within each chapter. All content carved from MASTER.

court_mechanism.description: Identical to RDC_WORKFLOW court mechanism. When SCRIBE flags a contradiction between source docs with no clear temporal supersession, the conflicting concept is resolved in a COURT session. Four advocates (2 per side) produce independent briefs; QUEEN rules.
court_mechanism.triggered_by: SCRIBE flags a conflict during upsert
court_mechanism.batching: conflicts accumulate during SCRIBE_LOOP; COURT phase processes them all between SCRIBE_LOOP and COMPOSITOR
court_mechanism.why_two_per_side: Redundancy strengthens the case. Convergence = strong side. Divergence = richer evidence. Internal disagreement = weak side.
court_mechanism.advocate_discipline: Advocates argue their assigned side regardless of personal assessment. Concession note optional.
decision_criteria_ordered: 1. Explicit supersession — later source doc explicitly says 'replaces X'
2. Temporal primacy — absent explicit supersession, later doc wins
3. Evidentiary weight — brief with more and better citations wins
4. Conceptual consistency — the value that fits the rest of MASTER best wins
5. Load-bearing-ness — if one value underlies more other content, it wins on inertia

ruling_options.SIDE_A_WINS: MASTER gets value A; side B noted as superseded in PEDAGOGY
ruling_options.SIDE_B_WINS: MASTER gets value B; side A noted as superseded in PEDAGOGY
ruling_options.SYNTHESIS: QUEEN interjects: neither side cleanly right; MASTER gets a synthesized value. Rationale required.
ruling_options.DEFER: Conflict reveals deeper question beyond court scope. Kicked to COMPOSITOR or escalated to human.
ruling_options.REJECT_BOTH: Both sides are wrong; MASTER marks concept unresolved. Triggers ESCALATE.
ruling_options.NO_DECISION: QUEEN cannot rule; criteria tied. Triggers ESCALATE to human.

recording.master_back_reference: Each court-resolved concept in MASTER.md gets a back-reference to the INPROGRESS court entry
recording.pedagogy_cross_reference: PEDAGOGY.md gets a short entry noting the court happened with link to INPROGRESS.md

court_mechanism.automatic_continuation: On SIDE_A_WINS, SIDE_B_WINS, or SYNTHESIS: QUEEN applies ruling and continues. On DEFER-to-compositor: mark concept for compositor attention. On DEFER-to-human, REJECT_BOTH, NO_DECISION: escalate and pause.

QUEEN.description: QUEEN is Claude in the main conversation when BOOK_CONSOLIDATION is active. Runs inventory, spawns SCRIBE sequentially, presides over COURT, spawns COMPOSITOR, spawns QA, manages MASTER lifecycle.
responsibilities: INVENTORY: list source files, determine temporal sort order, produce INVENTORY.md, await human confirmation
SCRIBE_LOOP orchestration: spawn SCRIBE once per source doc in temporal order
COURT adjudication: preside over court sessions for flagged conflicts
COMPOSITOR spawning: after SCRIBE_LOOP + COURT, spawn COMPOSITOR with final MASTER
QA spawning: after COMPOSITOR, spawn QA_COMPLETENESS then QA_COHERENCE
verdict execution: act on QA verdicts
escalation to human on loop limits, court escalations, or ESCALATE verdict

SCRIBE.description: Core upsert worker. Identical mechanics to RDC SCRIBE. Spawned once per source document in temporal order. Reads current MASTER + one source doc. Upserts all concepts into MASTER. Logs changes to PEDAGOGY. Produces evaluation entry. Flags contradictions for COURT.
inputs: source doc (single doc for this pass)
produces: MASTER.md (updated — concepts upserted, new concepts inserted, conflicts marked)
EVALUATIONS.md entry (appended — what this doc contributed)
upsert_rules: new concept not in MASTER → INSERT into structurally appropriate location
revised concept already in MASTER → OVERWRITE in place, log prior value to PEDAGOGY
identical concept → NO-OP, note as unchanged in evaluation
contradicting concept without clear temporal supersession → FLAG for COURT, insert both with conflict marker
NEVER delete from MASTER — overwrite only; deprecated concepts marked deprecated, not removed

ADVOCATE.description: Court advocate. Identical to RDC ADVOCATE. Given a conflicting concept and assigned side, produces a brief arguing for that side. Spawned 4 per court session (2 per side).
inputs: assigned side (A or B) with candidate value
produces: brief: structured argument for assigned side
ADVOCATE.discipline: advocate assigned side regardless of personal assessment
triggered_by: QUEEN during COURT phase — 4 spawned in parallel per session

COMPOSITOR.description: Replaces RDC's TAXONOMIST. Takes final MASTER.md (after all SCRIBE passes and COURT resolutions) and carves it into a structured manuscript. Discovers chapter structure from MASTER content — chapters are discovered, not predetermined. Produces ordered chapter files with section hierarchy and STRUCTURE.md as the manuscript skeleton.
inputs: MASTER.md (final state after all SCRIBE passes and COURT resolutions)
INPROGRESS.md court entries (for understanding resolved conflicts)
produces: STRUCTURE.md (TOC + chapter summaries + section listings + dependency map)
chapter_discovery_rules: chapters are discovered from MASTER content, not predetermined
chapter boundaries reflect natural conceptual coherence and logical grouping
chapter ordering reflects conceptual dependency (chapter N+1 builds on chapter N)
each chapter is a coherent unit — readable as a self-contained section within the larger work
every concept from MASTER lands in exactly one chapter — no duplication, no loss
related concepts are placed in the same or adjacent chapters
section hierarchy within chapters reflects logical sub-grouping of concepts
STRUCTURE.md dependency map must be acyclic — no circular chapter dependencies

QA_COMPLETENESS.description: Reviews ALL source documents against carved chapter files. Every concept that appeared in any source doc must be present in either (a) chapter files, (b) PEDAGOGY.md as superseded/deprecated, or (c) INPROGRESS.md as court-resolved. Missing concepts are flagged.
produces: completeness report: list of all concepts checked, status (present | superseded_in_pedagogy | court_resolved | MISSING)
if any MISSING: list with source doc reference and concept description

QA_COHERENCE.description: Structural coherence check on carved chapter files. Adapted from RDC QA_COHERENCE for manuscript structure.
produces: categories: chapter_ordering_errors | orphaned_concepts | section_misplacement | structure_md_inconsistencies | dependency_violations | conceptual_fragmentation
checks: do chapters follow a logical ordering (conceptual dependency respected)?
are there orphaned concepts (in MASTER but not in any chapter)?
do sections belong in their chapters (no misplaced content)?
is STRUCTURE.md consistent with actual chapter files (no phantom entries, no missing chapters)?
are related concepts co-located (same or adjacent chapters)?
is the manuscript structurally ready for storyboarding?

COURT_UNIT.execution: advocates spawned in parallel (4 at once); QUEEN ruling sequential after all 4 briefs return
COURT_UNIT.description: One court session per flagged conflict. Identical to RDC COURT_UNIT.

QA_UNIT.description: Both QA passes run in sequence. Completeness first, then coherence. Both must pass for GREEN_LIGHT.

actions: list all files in source directory
pre-read each file: extract filename, date/timestamp headers, creation metadata, first ~100 lines for context
determine temporal sort order: prefer explicit timestamps > filename conventions > file metadata > content-internal date references
produce INVENTORY.md: ordered manifest with reading sequence, per-file summary, detected epoch
initialize MASTER.md as empty structured document
initialize PEDAGOGY.md with header block
initialize EVALUATIONS.md with header block
ensure INPROGRESS.md exists at project root (create skeleton if missing)
report inventory to human: N files found, proposed reading order, await confirmation or override

scribe_loop.description: QUEEN iterates through INVENTORY in temporal order. For each source doc: spawn SCRIBE with current MASTER + doc. SCRIBE returns updated MASTER + PEDAGOGY delta + evaluation entry. QUEEN verifies, appends to INPROGRESS, advances.
per_iteration: QUEEN assembles context packet: current MASTER.md + source doc[i] + INVENTORY.md + PEDAGOGY.md + WORKER_SCRIBE.md + WORKER_PROTOCOL.md
SCRIBE returns: updated MASTER.md + PEDAGOGY.md append + EVALUATIONS.md entry
QUEEN writes SCRIBE report to INPROGRESS.md (prepend dated block)
scribe_loop.subset_awareness: when chapter_subset is specified, QUEEN constructs the SCRIBE_LOOP from only source files mapped to subset chapters (from triage.per_chapter_state[i].source_files for each chapter in the subset); source files that span multiple chapters (including both subset and non-subset chapters) are included but SCRIBE is given the chapter_subset as a focus directive so that upsert operations preferentially handle content relevant to the subset chapters
scribe_loop.completion: after final doc processed, QUEEN reports SCRIBE_LOOP complete with summary stats

court.description: Runs only if SCRIBE_LOOP flagged conflicts. Identical mechanics to RDC COURT.
court.skip_condition: if SCRIBE_LOOP flagged zero conflicts, COURT phase is skipped entirely
per_session: QUEEN reads conflict marker from MASTER
QUEEN assembles context packets for 4 advocates
QUEEN spawns all 4 ADVOCATEs in parallel
QUEEN applies decision criteria in priority order
QUEEN updates MASTER.md: replaces conflict marker with ruled value + back-reference
QUEEN advances to next conflict or ends COURT phase
court.on_escalation: NO_DECISION, REJECT_BOTH, or DEFER-to-human: QUEEN pauses, reports to human, waits

actions: QUEEN assembles context packet: final MASTER.md + PEDAGOGY.md + EVALUATIONS.md + INPROGRESS.md + BOOK_MANIFEST.json + WORKER_COMPOSITOR.md + WORKER_PROTOCOL.md

if chapter_subset is specified: include chapter_subset in context packet as a carving scope directive — COMPOSITOR carves only the subset chapters from MASTER; existing STRUCTURE.md entries for non-subset chapters are preserved
QUEEN spawns COMPOSITOR
COMPOSITOR returns: chapters/CH_<NN>_<TITLE>.md set + STRUCTURE.md
QUEEN writes COMPOSITOR report to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN proceeds to QA
QUEEN spawns QA_COMPLETENESS with: all source docs (subset-scoped if chapter_subset is specified) + all chapter files (subset only if chapter_subset) + STRUCTURE.md + PEDAGOGY + INPROGRESS + MASTER
QA_COMPLETENESS returns completeness report
QUEEN spawns QA_COHERENCE with: all chapter files (subset only if chapter_subset) + STRUCTURE.md + MASTER
QA_COHERENCE returns coherence report + verdict recommendation
QUEEN evaluates combined QA results → determines verdict
qa.subset_scoping_note: when chapter_subset is specified, both QA workers scope their checks to subset chapters and their associated source files only; subset scoping is noted in both QA reports

main_sequence: INVENTORY: QUEEN scans source directory, builds temporal manifest, awaits human confirmation
SCRIBE_LOOP: sequential upsert passes, one per source doc in temporal order
COURT: (conditional) resolve flagged conflicts via 4-advocate sessions
COMPOSITION: COMPOSITOR carves MASTER into chapter files + STRUCTURE.md
QA_UNIT: QA_COMPLETENESS then QA_COHERENCE review output set

GREEN_LIGHT.trigger: QA_COMPLETENESS finds zero MISSING concepts AND QA_COHERENCE finds zero structural issues
actions: QUEEN writes completion summary to INPROGRESS.md
output files finalized: chapters/, STRUCTURE.md, PEDAGOGY.md, MASTER.md, EVALUATIONS.md, INVENTORY.md
QUEEN commits output to project root
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_CONSOLIDATION complete. Structured manuscript ready for BOOK_STORYBOARD.'
QUEEN identifies which source doc(s) contain missing concepts
QUEEN re-spawns SCRIBE with: current MASTER + flagged source doc(s) + QA findings as focus directive
SCRIBE performs targeted upsert
QUEEN re-spawns COMPOSITOR (RECOMPOSE) to re-carve with updated MASTER
QUEEN re-spawns full QA_UNIT
qa_cycle_counter++

RECOMPOSE.trigger: QA_COMPLETENESS passes (no missing concepts) BUT QA_COHERENCE finds structural issues
actions: QUEEN re-spawns COMPOSITOR with: current MASTER + QA_COHERENCE findings as correction directive
COMPOSITOR re-carves chapter files and STRUCTURE.md addressing flagged issues
QUEEN re-spawns full QA_UNIT
qa_cycle_counter++

ESCALATE.trigger: qa_cycle_counter >= 3 OR court session rules NO_DECISION/REJECT_BOTH/DEFER-to-human OR unresolvable issue
actions: QUEEN writes escalation summary to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN pauses all worker spawning
QUEEN reports to human with structured escalation report
QUEEN waits for human direction
ESCALATE.resumption: human resolves; QUEEN resumes from appropriate point

counter_semantics.qa_cycle_counter: increments on any QA loop-back (SCRIBE_REVISIT or RECOMPOSE). At 3, auto-escalate. Shared across both loop-back types.

loop_limits.court_sessions_per_concept: no hard limit, but escalation blocks progress until resolved

context_access.note: no cleanroom roles — all workers need full visibility for consolidation

notes.rdc_fork_note: This workflow is a direct fork of RDC_WORKFLOW v1.1.0. SCRIBE, COURT, ADVOCATE, PEDAGOGY, EVALUATIONS, INVENTORY mechanics are identical. The only structural difference is COMPOSITOR (replaces TAXONOMIST) and the output shape (chapters instead of PHASE/ARCH/TODO). Future refactoring may extract shared mechanics into a base workflow.
notes.manifest_note: BOOK_MANIFEST.json is read at engagement for genre/structure context. COMPOSITOR uses manifest genre and structure hints when discovering chapter boundaries. The manifest is not modified by this workflow.
notes.master_preservation_note: MASTER.md is preserved at project root after GREEN_LIGHT. It is not consumed by downstream BOOK workflows but serves as a single-file project reference and enables future re-consolidation runs.
notes.chapter_naming_note: Chapter files use zero-padded numbering (CH_01, CH_02, ...) for filesystem ordering. TITLE is a short descriptive slug derived from chapter content, not the source doc filename.
